articles.headline: India-Africa Economic Corridor: Significant Progress Reported in Key Infrastructure Projects
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of External Affairs today highlighted substantial advancements in the India-Africa Economic Corridor (IAEC) initiative, particularly in connectivity and digital infrastructure projects.
• This corridor, building on the G20 Delhi Declaration, aims to bolster trade, investment, and people-to-people linkages between India and African nations.
• Focus areas include enhancing port connectivity, developing trans-continental highways, and establishing robust digital payment systems and fiber optic networks.
• India's commitment to capacity building and skill development programs in partner African countries was also reaffirmed.
• The initiative is seen as a strategic move to foster South-South cooperation and provide an alternative development model.

articles.headline: Parliament Winter Session Commences: Focus on Economic Reforms and Data Protection Bill
articles.explainer:
• The Winter Session of Parliament began today, with key legislative business including several economic reform bills and the much-anticipated Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Bill amendments.
• Opposition parties are expected to raise concerns over rising inflation, unemployment, and issues related to federalism, setting the stage for a contentious session.
• The government aims to push for faster implementation of its 'Vision 2047' agenda, with specific bills targeting ease of doing business and investment.
• Discussions are also anticipated on the status of various social welfare schemes and the national security implications of emerging technologies.
• The Speaker appealed for constructive debate and cooperation from all members to ensure productive legislative outcomes.

articles.trivia: The first session of the Constituent Assembly of India was held on December 9, 1946.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Global Uncertainties and Domestic Inflation Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) today announced its decision to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged, citing persistent global economic uncertainties and the need to vigilantly manage domestic inflationary pressures.
• The MPC emphasized its commitment to achieving the medium-term target for consumer price index (CPI) inflation of 4 percent within a band of +/- 2 percent.
• While core inflation has shown signs of moderation, volatile food prices remain a concern, influencing the committee's cautious stance.
• The RBI reiterated its accommodative stance while maintaining readiness to act if economic conditions warrant a change in policy direction.
• Analysts suggest this decision reflects a balancing act between supporting economic growth and anchoring inflation expectations.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth

mainsPoints: Discuss the role and effectiveness of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in managing inflation and supporting economic growth in India.
Analyze the factors that influence the RBI's decision-making process regarding interest rates, particularly in the context of global economic fluctuations and domestic challenges.

articles.imageDescription: Logo of the Reserve Bank of India.
articles.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, in accordance with the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

articles.headline: India-US Joint Statement Emphasizes Quad's Role in Indo-Pacific Maritime Security
articles.explainer:
• Following bilateral talks, India and the United States issued a joint statement reaffirming their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region, with a strong emphasis on maritime security cooperation within the Quad framework.
• Both nations underscored the importance of upholding international law, particularly the UN Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS), in resolving maritime disputes.
• The discussion covered joint exercises, information sharing, and capacity-building initiatives with regional partners to counter threats like piracy, illegal fishing, and natural disasters.
• The statement also touched upon enhancing resilience of supply chains and promoting secure technology ecosystems in the region.
• This reiteration comes amidst increasing geopolitical complexities and strategic competition in the Indo-Pacific.

articles.headline: Government Unveils Phase-II of National Logistics Policy: Aiming for Global Competitiveness
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government today launched the second phase of its ambitious National Logistics Policy, focusing on integrating advanced technologies and streamlining regulatory frameworks to reduce logistics costs.
• This phase emphasizes the development of multimodal logistics parks, leveraging Artificial Intelligence (AI) for supply chain optimization, and promoting green logistics solutions.
• The policy aims to bring down logistics costs from the current high levels to a global benchmark of less than 8% of GDP by 2030.
• Skill development for logistics professionals and creation of a unified digital platform for end-to-end logistics management are also key components.
• The move is expected to significantly boost India's manufacturing competitiveness and export potential.

articles.trivia: India's logistics sector is one of the largest in the world, employing over 22 million people.

articles.headline: Supreme Court to Review Petitions Challenging Electoral Bond Scheme
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court today announced it would commence hearing a batch of petitions challenging the constitutionality of the Electoral Bond Scheme, a topic of significant public and political debate.
• Petitioners argue that the scheme lacks transparency, potentially leading to quid pro quo arrangements and undue corporate influence in politics, thereby violating the 'right to know' of citizens.
• The government has defended the scheme as a measure to curb black money in political funding and ensure donor anonymity.
• The court's decision is expected to have far-reaching implications for electoral finance and transparency in India's democratic process.
• The bench highlighted the importance of balancing donor privacy with public interest in transparency.

articles.trivia: The first general elections in India were held from 1951 to 1952.

articles.headline: Government Boosts Climate-Resilient Agriculture Initiatives Ahead of COP30
articles.explainer:
• With COP30 on the horizon, the Union Ministry of Agriculture announced enhanced funding and new programs to promote climate-resilient agriculture practices across India.
• The initiatives focus on developing drought-resistant crop varieties, improving water management techniques, and promoting organic farming and agroforestry.
• Emphasis is also placed on capacity building for farmers to adopt sustainable practices and integrate climate information services into their decision-making.
• The move is crucial for ensuring food security amidst changing weather patterns and achieving India's climate action commitments.
• Public-private partnerships are being encouraged to scale up the implementation of these innovative farming methods.

articles.headline: ISRO Gears Up for Testing of Next-Gen Launch Vehicle (NGLV) Components
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the commencement of critical component testing for its Next-Generation Launch Vehicle (NGLV), aimed at making India's space access more economical and frequent.
• NGLV is envisioned as a heavy-lift, reusable rocket, capable of carrying multiple payloads to various orbits, including geostationary transfer orbit (GTO).
• The design incorporates advanced propulsion systems and modular architecture to enhance flexibility and reduce turnaround time between missions.
• This development is crucial for meeting the growing demands of India's space economy and global satellite launch market.
• ISRO is collaborating with private sector players for manufacturing and operational support, fostering a vibrant space ecosystem.

articles.headline: National Digital Health Mission Expands Reach: Focus on Telemedicine and EHR Integration
articles.explainer:
• The National Digital Health Mission (NDHM), now known as Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM), announced its expansion to integrate more public and private healthcare providers, with a strong focus on telemedicine services and electronic health records (EHR) integration.
• The mission aims to create a seamless online platform for patients to access health services, manage their health records, and connect with healthcare professionals across the country.
• Key components include health ID, Healthcare Professionals Registry (HPR), and Health Facility Registry (HFR).
• This expansion is critical for improving healthcare accessibility, especially in remote areas, and enhancing the efficiency of the healthcare system.
• Data privacy and security remain paramount, with robust frameworks being implemented to protect patient information.

articles.headline: India's Green Hydrogen Mission Shows Promising Early Results; Investment Inflow Increases
articles.explainer:
• The National Green Hydrogen Mission is reporting promising early results, with significant private sector investment flowing into electrolyzer manufacturing and green hydrogen production projects.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization goals.
• Key policy enablers include incentives for production, development of green hydrogen corridors, and R&D support for cost reduction.
• The government expects green hydrogen to play a crucial role in decarbonizing hard-to-abate sectors like steel, refining, and fertilizers.
• This progress is vital for India to achieve its net-zero emissions target by 2070 and reduce reliance on fossil fuel imports.

articles.headline: India Reaffirms Stance on Indus Waters Treaty Amidst Ongoing Deliberations
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of External Affairs today reiterated India's commitment to the provisions of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) while emphasizing the need for good faith implementation by both parties, amidst ongoing deliberations with Pakistan.
• India has previously sought modifications to the treaty, citing concerns over Pakistan's objections to certain Indian hydropower projects on western rivers.
• The IWT, brokered by the World Bank in 1960, allocates the waters of the Indus river system between India and Pakistan.
• The treaty is considered one of the most successful water-sharing agreements globally, despite periodic tensions between the two nations.
• Discussions are focused on resolving technical issues and ensuring mutual cooperation for optimal water resource management.

articles.trivia: The Indus River is one of the longest rivers in Asia, originating in the Tibetan Plateau.

articles.headline: National Urban Flood Resilience Plan Launched to Mitigate Impact of Extreme Weather Events
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs today launched the National Urban Flood Resilience Plan, a comprehensive strategy to enhance the preparedness and response capabilities of Indian cities against increasing urban flooding.
• The plan integrates early warning systems, improved drainage infrastructure, sustainable urban planning, and community engagement for disaster management.
• It emphasizes the adoption of nature-based solutions like permeable pavements and urban wetlands to absorb excess rainwater.
• The initiative is a direct response to the escalating frequency and intensity of extreme rainfall events exacerbated by climate change.
• Funding mechanisms and technical assistance will be provided to states and urban local bodies for effective implementation.

articles.headline: INS Vikrant Set for Major Deployment in Indo-Pacific: Boosting India's Naval Prowess
articles.explainer:
• India's first indigenously-built aircraft carrier, INS Vikrant, is reportedly preparing for its first major operational deployment in the Indo-Pacific region, marking a significant milestone for the Indian Navy.
• This deployment underscores India's growing naval capabilities and its commitment to safeguarding maritime interests and promoting regional stability.
• INS Vikrant's operationalization enhances India's 'blue water' capabilities, allowing power projection far beyond its coastal waters.
• The carrier will host a mix of MiG-29K fighter jets, helicopters, and potentially indigenous naval combat aircraft in the future.
• This move aligns with India's broader vision of becoming a net security provider in the Indian Ocean Region.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Unveils National Framework for AI Ethics and Governance
articles.explainer:
• NITI Aayog today launched a comprehensive National Framework for AI Ethics and Governance, aiming to guide the responsible development and deployment of Artificial Intelligence (AI) technologies in India.
• The framework emphasizes principles such as fairness, transparency, accountability, privacy, and safety in AI systems.
• It seeks to balance innovation with ethical considerations, ensuring that AI contributes positively to societal well-being and economic growth.
• The framework also proposes a multi-stakeholder approach involving government, industry, academia, and civil society for its continuous evolution and implementation.
• This initiative positions India as a leader in responsible AI development on the global stage.

articles.headline: New National Tourism Policy Prioritizes Sustainable and Responsible Tourism
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism today unveiled its revised National Tourism Policy, placing a strong emphasis on sustainable and responsible tourism practices to protect India's natural and cultural heritage.
• The policy aims to promote eco-tourism, community-based tourism, and develop niche tourism products while ensuring minimal environmental impact.
• It includes provisions for skill development in the tourism sector, digital promotion, and enhancing connectivity to lesser-known destinations.
• The goal is to position India as a leading global destination for experiential and mindful travel, contributing to local economies and employment.
• Public-private partnerships and active involvement of local communities are central to the policy's implementation.

articles.headline: Namami Gange Mission Phase-II: Focus on Decentralized Wastewater Treatment and Riverine Biodiversity
articles.explainer:
• The second phase of the Namami Gange Mission is intensifying efforts, with a renewed focus on decentralized wastewater treatment systems and conservation of riverine biodiversity across the Ganga basin.
• The mission aims to achieve 'Aviral Dhara' (uninterrupted flow) and 'Nirmal Dhara' (clean flow) of the Ganga by 2030, building on the achievements of the first phase.
• New projects include wetland restoration, afforestation along the river banks, and promoting sustainable agricultural practices to reduce pollution.
• Community participation and public awareness campaigns are being scaled up to ensure long-term sustainability of the rejuvenation efforts.
• This holistic approach addresses both pollution abatement and ecological restoration of the national river.

articles.headline: Government Strengthens Cybersecurity Framework for Critical National Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• In response to evolving cyber threats, the National Cyber Security Coordinator's office announced a significant upgrade to the cybersecurity framework protecting India's critical national infrastructure (CNI).
• The enhanced framework includes mandatory audits, real-time threat intelligence sharing, and stricter compliance standards for sectors like energy, finance, telecom, and transportation.
• Emphasis is placed on capacity building for cybersecurity professionals and promoting indigenous cybersecurity solutions.
• This move is crucial for safeguarding essential services and national security from state-sponsored attacks and cyber warfare.
• Public-private collaboration is being intensified to create a resilient and robust cyber defense ecosystem.

articles.headline: Pilot Urban Employment Guarantee Scheme Launched in Select Cities
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Labour and Employment today initiated a pilot Urban Employment Guarantee Scheme in a few selected cities, aiming to provide livelihood support to urban poor.
• Modelled on the lines of MGNREGA, the scheme guarantees a minimum number of days of wage employment for unskilled and semi-skilled workers.
• The program focuses on urban infrastructure development, sanitation, and environmental improvement projects.
• This initiative seeks to address the growing issue of urban unemployment and provide a social safety net, especially for vulnerable populations.
• Lessons learned from the pilot phase will inform the potential nationwide rollout of the scheme.

articles.headline: BharatNet Phase-III Expansion Underway: Bridging Digital Divide in Rural India
articles.explainer:
• The Department of Telecommunications announced the commencement of BharatNet Phase-III, focusing on extending high-speed broadband connectivity to all gram panchayats and remote villages across India.
• This phase involves upgrading existing infrastructure, deploying advanced fiber optic networks, and leveraging satellite communication for difficult terrains.
• The initiative is crucial for achieving digital inclusion, enabling e-governance services, digital education, and telemedicine in rural areas.
• Public-private partnership models are being explored to expedite deployment and ensure sustainable operations.
• BharatNet is a cornerstone of the 'Digital India' vision, aiming to bridge the urban-rural digital divide.

articles.trivia: India is the world's second-largest telecommunications market.

articles.headline: ASI Announces Major Heritage Conservation and Restoration Projects Across India
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) today unveiled a series of major conservation and restoration projects for several historically significant sites across the country.
• These projects will focus on structural preservation, scientific cleaning, and enhancing visitor facilities at key archaeological monuments and heritage complexes.
• The initiative aims to protect India's rich cultural heritage, promote heritage tourism, and educate the public about historical significance.
• Advanced techniques, including laser cleaning and 3D mapping, will be employed to ensure meticulous restoration.
• Community involvement and skill development for local artisans in traditional restoration techniques are also integral to the program.

articles.trivia: The Archaeological Survey of India was founded in 1861 by Alexander Cunningham.

articles.headline: Animal Welfare Board Issues New Guidelines for Protection of Street Animals
articles.explainer:
• The Animal Welfare Board of India (AWBI) today issued a comprehensive set of new guidelines aimed at enhancing the protection and welfare of street animals across urban and rural areas.
• The guidelines emphasize responsible pet ownership, promotion of animal birth control (ABC) programs, and stricter penalties for animal cruelty.
• They also call for better coordination between local authorities, NGOs, and community volunteers in managing street animal populations and providing veterinary care.
• The move comes amidst increasing concerns over animal cruelty and the need for a more humane approach to animal management.
• Public awareness campaigns will be launched to foster empathy and responsible behavior towards animals.

articles.headline: G20 Sherpa Meeting Underway: Paving Way for Next Leaders' Summit Agenda
articles.explainer:
• The G20 Sherpa meeting is currently underway, laying the groundwork for the agenda and key deliverables of the upcoming G20 Leaders' Summit.
• Discussions are focusing on global economic recovery, climate finance, digital transformation, and sustainable development goals.
• India, as a significant member, is actively contributing to shaping the global narrative, advocating for the interests of the Global South and pushing for reforms in multilateral institutions.
• The Sherpas are negotiating the draft communiqué, which will outline the collective commitments of G20 nations.
• This preparatory meeting is crucial for building consensus on complex global issues before the leaders convene.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the India-Africa Economic Corridor (IAEC), consider the following statements:
1. It is an initiative primarily focused on military cooperation between India and African nations.
2. The corridor aims to enhance trade, investment, and connectivity through physical and digital infrastructure.
3. The G20 Delhi Declaration highlighted the importance of such economic corridors.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. The IAEC primarily focuses on economic, trade, and connectivity aspects, not military cooperation. Statements 2 and 3 are correct. The IAEC aims to bolster trade, investment, and people-to-people linkages through infrastructure, and the G20 Delhi Declaration indeed emphasized such economic corridors.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is the primary objective of the Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)?
options: (a) To manage the government's fiscal deficit.
(b) To maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
(c) To regulate foreign exchange reserves for trade balance.
(d) To promote financial inclusion and digital payments.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) To maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The primary objective of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is to maintain price stability, specifically targeting a Consumer Price Index (CPI) inflation of 4 percent within a band of +/- 2 percent, while simultaneously considering the objective of economic growth.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the National Green Hydrogen Mission:
1. Its primary goal is to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
2. Green hydrogen is produced using fossil fuels with carbon capture technology.
3. The mission aims to decarbonize hard-to-abate sectors like steel and refining.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The mission aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen. Statement 2 is incorrect. Green hydrogen is produced using renewable energy sources (like solar, wind) through electrolysis, not fossil fuels with carbon capture (which would be blue hydrogen). Statement 3 is correct. Decarbonizing hard-to-abate sectors is a key objective.

prelimsMocks.question: The Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) was brokered by which international organization?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) was brokered by the World Bank in 1960 between India and Pakistan, allocating the waters of the Indus river system.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is the premier organization for archaeological research and heritage conservation in India?
options: (d) National Centre for Cultural Resources and Training (NCCRT)
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) is the premier organization under the Ministry of Culture for archaeological research and the protection of cultural heritage, including monuments of national importance.

mainsMocks.question: Discuss the strategic implications of the India-Africa Economic Corridor (IAEC) for India's foreign policy and economic interests. How does this initiative contribute to South-South cooperation and India's role in a multipolar world? (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce IAEC and its objectives. Discuss strategic implications like enhanced geopolitical influence, counterbalancing other powers, and strengthening ties with African nations. Highlight economic benefits such as market access, resource security, and infrastructure development. Conclude on its role in South-South cooperation and a multipolar world order.

mainsMocks.question: Critically examine the Electoral Bond Scheme in India, weighing its stated objectives against concerns regarding transparency and democratic accountability. In light of the Supreme Court's review, suggest alternative mechanisms for political funding that can ensure both transparency and discourage the flow of black money. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution; Separation of powers; Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin with the Electoral Bond Scheme's introduction and stated objectives (curbing black money, transparency). Then, critically analyze the concerns (anonymity, corporate influence, violation of right to know). Discuss the Supreme Court's role. Finally, propose alternative reforms like state funding, electoral trusts, and stricter disclosure norms.

mainsMocks.question: Analyze the objectives and key components of India's National Logistics Policy and its potential impact on the country's economic growth and global competitiveness. Discuss how technological interventions, such as AI and digital platforms, can transform India's logistics sector and address existing bottlenecks. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.; Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the National Logistics Policy and its aim to reduce logistics costs. Detail its components like multimodal transport, infrastructure development, and skill enhancement. Explain how reduced logistics costs boost manufacturing and exports. Discuss the transformative role of AI, IoT, and ULIP in optimizing supply chains, improving efficiency, and addressing challenges like last-mile connectivity and inventory management.

GS2.headline: India-Africa Economic Corridor: Significant Progress Reported in Key Infrastructure Projects
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of External Affairs today highlighted substantial advancements in the India-Africa Economic Corridor (IAEC) initiative, particularly in connectivity and digital infrastructure projects.
• This corridor, building on the G20 Delhi Declaration, aims to bolster trade, investment, and people-to-people linkages between India and African nations.
• Focus areas include enhancing port connectivity, developing trans-continental highways, and establishing robust digital payment systems and fiber optic networks.
• India's commitment to capacity building and skill development programs in partner African countries was also reaffirmed.
• The initiative is seen as a strategic move to foster South-South cooperation and provide an alternative development model.

GS2.headline: Parliament Winter Session Commences: Focus on Economic Reforms and Data Protection Bill
GS2.explainer:
• The Winter Session of Parliament began today, with key legislative business including several economic reform bills and the much-anticipated Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Bill amendments.
• Opposition parties are expected to raise concerns over rising inflation, unemployment, and issues related to federalism, setting the stage for a contentious session.
• The government aims to push for faster implementation of its 'Vision 2047' agenda, with specific bills targeting ease of doing business and investment.
• Discussions are also anticipated on the status of various social welfare schemes and the national security implications of emerging technologies.
• The Speaker appealed for constructive debate and cooperation from all members to ensure productive legislative outcomes.

GS2.headline: India-US Joint Statement Emphasizes Quad's Role in Indo-Pacific Maritime Security
GS2.explainer:
• Following bilateral talks, India and the United States issued a joint statement reaffirming their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region, with a strong emphasis on maritime security cooperation within the Quad framework.
• Both nations underscored the importance of upholding international law, particularly the UN Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS), in resolving maritime disputes.
• The discussion covered joint exercises, information sharing, and capacity-building initiatives with regional partners to counter threats like piracy, illegal fishing, and natural disasters.
• The statement also touched upon enhancing resilience of supply chains and promoting secure technology ecosystems in the region.
• This reiteration comes amidst increasing geopolitical complexities and strategic competition in the Indo-Pacific.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court to Review Petitions Challenging Electoral Bond Scheme
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court today announced it would commence hearing a batch of petitions challenging the constitutionality of the Electoral Bond Scheme, a topic of significant public and political debate.
• Petitioners argue that the scheme lacks transparency, potentially leading to quid pro quo arrangements and undue corporate influence in politics, thereby violating the 'right to know' of citizens.
• The government has defended the scheme as a measure to curb black money in political funding and ensure donor anonymity.
• The court's decision is expected to have far-reaching implications for electoral finance and transparency in India's democratic process.
• The bench highlighted the importance of balancing donor privacy with public interest in transparency.

GS2.headline: National Digital Health Mission Expands Reach: Focus on Telemedicine and EHR Integration
GS2.explainer:
• The National Digital Health Mission (NDHM), now known as Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM), announced its expansion to integrate more public and private healthcare providers, with a strong focus on telemedicine services and electronic health records (EHR) integration.
• The mission aims to create a seamless online platform for patients to access health services, manage their health records, and connect with healthcare professionals across the country.
• Key components include health ID, Healthcare Professionals Registry (HPR), and Health Facility Registry (HFR).
• This expansion is critical for improving healthcare accessibility, especially in remote areas, and enhancing the efficiency of the healthcare system.
• Data privacy and security remain paramount, with robust frameworks being implemented to protect patient information.

GS2.headline: India Reaffirms Stance on Indus Waters Treaty Amidst Ongoing Deliberations
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of External Affairs today reiterated India's commitment to the provisions of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) while emphasizing the need for good faith implementation by both parties, amidst ongoing deliberations with Pakistan.
• India has previously sought modifications to the treaty, citing concerns over Pakistan's objections to certain Indian hydropower projects on western rivers.
• The IWT, brokered by the World Bank in 1960, allocates the waters of the Indus river system between India and Pakistan.
• The treaty is considered one of the most successful water-sharing agreements globally, despite periodic tensions between the two nations.
• Discussions are focused on resolving technical issues and ensuring mutual cooperation for optimal water resource management.

• Key agreements were signed to boost trade volumes, facilitate transit through the Chabahar Port, and explore new transport corridors.
• Discussions also focused on combating terrorism, drug trafficking, and promoting cultural exchanges to strengthen people-to-people ties.
• India reiterated its commitment to extending development assistance and capacity building programs to Central Asian nations.
• The summit aims to institutionalize India's engagement with the region, recognizing its strategic importance for energy security and regional stability.

GS2.headline: Pilot Urban Employment Guarantee Scheme Launched in Select Cities
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Labour and Employment today initiated a pilot Urban Employment Guarantee Scheme in a few selected cities, aiming to provide livelihood support to urban poor.
• Modelled on the lines of MGNREGA, the scheme guarantees a minimum number of days of wage employment for unskilled and semi-skilled workers.
• The program focuses on urban infrastructure development, sanitation, and environmental improvement projects.
• This initiative seeks to address the growing issue of urban unemployment and provide a social safety net, especially for vulnerable populations.
• Lessons learned from the pilot phase will inform the potential nationwide rollout of the scheme.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Global Uncertainties and Domestic Inflation Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) today announced its decision to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged, citing persistent global economic uncertainties and the need to vigilantly manage domestic inflationary pressures.
• The MPC emphasized its commitment to achieving the medium-term target for consumer price index (CPI) inflation of 4 percent within a band of +/- 2 percent.
• While core inflation has shown signs of moderation, volatile food prices remain a concern, influencing the committee's cautious stance.
• The RBI reiterated its accommodative stance while maintaining readiness to act if economic conditions warrant a change in policy direction.
• Analysts suggest this decision reflects a balancing act between supporting economic growth and anchoring inflation expectations.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils Phase-II of National Logistics Policy: Aiming for Global Competitiveness
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government today launched the second phase of its ambitious National Logistics Policy, focusing on integrating advanced technologies and streamlining regulatory frameworks to reduce logistics costs.
• This phase emphasizes the development of multimodal logistics parks, leveraging Artificial Intelligence (AI) for supply chain optimization, and promoting green logistics solutions.
• The policy aims to bring down logistics costs from the current high levels to a global benchmark of less than 8% of GDP by 2030.
• Skill development for logistics professionals and creation of a unified digital platform for end-to-end logistics management are also key components.
• The move is expected to significantly boost India's manufacturing competitiveness and export potential.

GS3.trivia: India's logistics sector is one of the largest in the world, employing over 22 million people.

GS3.headline: Government Boosts Climate-Resilient Agriculture Initiatives Ahead of COP30
GS3.explainer:
• With COP30 on the horizon, the Union Ministry of Agriculture announced enhanced funding and new programs to promote climate-resilient agriculture practices across India.
• The initiatives focus on developing drought-resistant crop varieties, improving water management techniques, and promoting organic farming and agroforestry.
• Emphasis is also placed on capacity building for farmers to adopt sustainable practices and integrate climate information services into their decision-making.
• The move is crucial for ensuring food security amidst changing weather patterns and achieving India's climate action commitments.
• Public-private partnerships are being encouraged to scale up the implementation of these innovative farming methods.

GS3.headline: ISRO Gears Up for Testing of Next-Gen Launch Vehicle (NGLV) Components
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the commencement of critical component testing for its Next-Generation Launch Vehicle (NGLV), aimed at making India's space access more economical and frequent.
• NGLV is envisioned as a heavy-lift, reusable rocket, capable of carrying multiple payloads to various orbits, including geostationary transfer orbit (GTO).
• The design incorporates advanced propulsion systems and modular architecture to enhance flexibility and reduce turnaround time between missions.
• This development is crucial for meeting the growing demands of India's space economy and global satellite launch market.
• ISRO is collaborating with private sector players for manufacturing and operational support, fostering a vibrant space ecosystem.

GS3.headline: India's Green Hydrogen Mission Shows Promising Early Results; Investment Inflow Increases
GS3.explainer:
• The National Green Hydrogen Mission is reporting promising early results, with significant private sector investment flowing into electrolyzer manufacturing and green hydrogen production projects.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization goals.
• Key policy enablers include incentives for production, development of green hydrogen corridors, and R&D support for cost reduction.
• The government expects green hydrogen to play a crucial role in decarbonizing hard-to-abate sectors like steel, refining, and fertilizers.
• This progress is vital for India to achieve its net-zero emissions target by 2070 and reduce reliance on fossil fuel imports.

GS3.headline: National Urban Flood Resilience Plan Launched to Mitigate Impact of Extreme Weather Events
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs today launched the National Urban Flood Resilience Plan, a comprehensive strategy to enhance the preparedness and response capabilities of Indian cities against increasing urban flooding.
• The plan integrates early warning systems, improved drainage infrastructure, sustainable urban planning, and community engagement for disaster management.
• It emphasizes the adoption of nature-based solutions like permeable pavements and urban wetlands to absorb excess rainwater.
• The initiative is a direct response to the escalating frequency and intensity of extreme rainfall events exacerbated by climate change.
• Funding mechanisms and technical assistance will be provided to states and urban local bodies for effective implementation.

GS3.headline: INS Vikrant Set for Major Deployment in Indo-Pacific: Boosting India's Naval Prowess
GS3.explainer:
• India's first indigenously-built aircraft carrier, INS Vikrant, is reportedly preparing for its first major operational deployment in the Indo-Pacific region, marking a significant milestone for the Indian Navy.
• This deployment underscores India's growing naval capabilities and its commitment to safeguarding maritime interests and promoting regional stability.
• INS Vikrant's operationalization enhances India's 'blue water' capabilities, allowing power projection far beyond its coastal waters.
• The carrier will host a mix of MiG-29K fighter jets, helicopters, and potentially indigenous naval combat aircraft in the future.
• This move aligns with India's broader vision of becoming a net security provider in the Indian Ocean Region.

GS3.headline: NITI Aayog Unveils National Framework for AI Ethics and Governance
GS3.explainer:
• NITI Aayog today launched a comprehensive National Framework for AI Ethics and Governance, aiming to guide the responsible development and deployment of Artificial Intelligence (AI) technologies in India.
• The framework emphasizes principles such as fairness, transparency, accountability, privacy, and safety in AI systems.
• It seeks to balance innovation with ethical considerations, ensuring that AI contributes positively to societal well-being and economic growth.
• The framework also proposes a multi-stakeholder approach involving government, industry, academia, and civil society for its continuous evolution and implementation.
• This initiative positions India as a leader in responsible AI development on the global stage.

GS3.headline: New National Tourism Policy Prioritizes Sustainable and Responsible Tourism
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism today unveiled its revised National Tourism Policy, placing a strong emphasis on sustainable and responsible tourism practices to protect India's natural and cultural heritage.
• The policy aims to promote eco-tourism, community-based tourism, and develop niche tourism products while ensuring minimal environmental impact.
• It includes provisions for skill development in the tourism sector, digital promotion, and enhancing connectivity to lesser-known destinations.
• The goal is to position India as a leading global destination for experiential and mindful travel, contributing to local economies and employment.
• Public-private partnerships and active involvement of local communities are central to the policy's implementation.

GS3.headline: Namami Gange Mission Phase-II: Focus on Decentralized Wastewater Treatment and Riverine Biodiversity
GS3.explainer:
• The second phase of the Namami Gange Mission is intensifying efforts, with a renewed focus on decentralized wastewater treatment systems and conservation of riverine biodiversity across the Ganga basin.
• The mission aims to achieve 'Aviral Dhara' (uninterrupted flow) and 'Nirmal Dhara' (clean flow) of the Ganga by 2030, building on the achievements of the first phase.
• New projects include wetland restoration, afforestation along the river banks, and promoting sustainable agricultural practices to reduce pollution.
• Community participation and public awareness campaigns are being scaled up to ensure long-term sustainability of the rejuvenation efforts.
• This holistic approach addresses both pollution abatement and ecological restoration of the national river.

GS3.headline: Government Strengthens Cybersecurity Framework for Critical National Infrastructure
GS3.explainer:
• In response to evolving cyber threats, the National Cyber Security Coordinator's office announced a significant upgrade to the cybersecurity framework protecting India's critical national infrastructure (CNI).
• The enhanced framework includes mandatory audits, real-time threat intelligence sharing, and stricter compliance standards for sectors like energy, finance, telecom, and transportation.
• Emphasis is placed on capacity building for cybersecurity professionals and promoting indigenous cybersecurity solutions.
• This move is crucial for safeguarding essential services and national security from state-sponsored attacks and cyber warfare.
• Public-private collaboration is being intensified to create a resilient and robust cyber defense ecosystem.
